svg.text: II. Differences Between Complex and Basic Creations

g.text: Complex Creations can be configured with a Creation Status Decision Node Graph.
It continuously accesses this graph and enters different autonomous logic statuses
based on configured conditions. Can reference the Creation Status Node Graph.
Complex Creations can use Custom Skills. Skills must be defined before use in the Status Node Graph.
Skill usage is subject to three cooldown timers: Global Skill CD, Skill CD, and Skill Group CD.
Referenced in the Status Node Graph for Skill execution
Custom Skills can only reference Skills the Creation can use
In Battle: starts when entering battle.
Out of Battle: starts when exiting battle.
Random time within range used as effective cooldown
Random time within range as Actual Cooldown Time
Skill can only be used when Skill CD + Global CD + Group CD ready
When Skill ends: start CD after skill finishes.
When Skill starts: start CD when skill begins.
Toggle whether this skill affects or ignores Global CD
All Skills for a Complex Creation are affected by Global Cooldown Time.
Links cooldown timers of multiple Skills. Skills in the same CD Group share cooldown timing.
Complex Creations require Autonomous Logic Parameter Templates applied in the Status Node Graph.
These parameters define the basic combat behavior requirements.
Initial Effective Autonomous Logic Parameter Template
Active by default at runtime. Maximum 1 template active initially.
Lists all templates configured for this Complex Creation. Add new templates (inactive by default).
Template_X where X is the template ID — used as Node input for state adjustment.
If enabled, template takes effect immediately when created
When hostiles in range, Creation can detect and enter battle
Toggle visual preview of Detection Range in editor
Nearby Creations also enter battle with target
Exceed distance from target → leaves battle
If no valid path to target, leave battle
Delay before leaving battle after pathfinding failure
None, Sphere, or Cylinder (size params required)
Refer to the Patrol section under General Settings for patrol configuration details.

svg.text: III. Managing Behaviors Through Local Filter

g.text: Starts with "Execute only by sequence" node. Each output pin connects to
"Switch to self execution status" node to execute different behaviors.
Continuously evaluates states in order. If a preceding state's conditions are met,
the Creation immediately switches to that state. If none match, no state executes.
Supports priority-based branching: branch 1 → branch 2 → branch N.
Input and output pins support only single-line connections.
Represents continuous behavior — while the Creation stays in a given state,
the configured behavior executes repeatedly until the Decision Graph switches state.

svg.text: Generated by AI based on official Miliastra Academy documentation. Version: Luminary
